content: CAR-T (chimeric antigen receptor T-cell) therapy is a form of adoptive cell immunotherapy. Patient T-cells are collected by leukapheresis, genetically engineered ex vivo to express CAR targeting tumor antigens, expanded, and reinfused after lymphodepleting chemotherapy. CAR structure: extracellular antigen-binding domain (scFv from monoclonal antibody), hinge region, transmembrane domain, intracellular signaling domains (CD3ζ plus costimulatory molecules CD28 or 4-1BB). FDA-approved products: tisagenlecleucel (Kymriah, CD19-targeted, B-cell ALL and DLBCL), axicabtagene ciloleucel (Yescarta, CD19, DLBCL and follicular lymphoma), lisocabtagene maraleucel (Breyanzi, CD19, DLBCL and CLL), brexucabtagene autoleucel (Tecartus, CD19, mantle cell lymphoma and ALL), idecabtagene vicleucel (Abecma, BCMA, myeloma), ciltacabtagene autoleucel (Carvykti, BCMA, myeloma). Targets: CD19 for B-cell malignancies; BCMA for multiple myeloma; investigational targets include CD22, CD30, GPC3, mesothelin.
